title: Multilayer film for packaging and method of manufacture thereof
abstract: Disclosed is a multilayer film for packaging. The multilayer film comprises a layer of ink and an adjacent layer of substrate. The layer of ink includes functionalised graphene and a material selected from cellulose, polylactic acid and a polyhydroxyalkanoate.
claims: Claims 1. A multilayer film (100) for packaging comprising : - a layer of ink (102) comprising functionalised graphene and a material selected from cellulose, polylactic acid and a polyhydroxyalkanoate; and - an adjacent layer of substrate (104). 2. A multilayer film (100, 200) of claim 1, further including a second layer of substrate (202) such that the layer of ink (102) is sandwiched between the layers of substrate (104, 202). 3. A multilayer film (100, 200, 300) of claim 2, further including a second layer of ink (302) between the second layer of substrate (202) and the layer of ink (102). 4. A multilayer (100, 200, 300) film of claim 1, 2 and 3, characterized in that each of the layers of ink (102, 302) and each of the layers of substrate (104, 202) includes a thickness in a range of 1 nanometres to 10 micrometres and a thickness in a range of 10 micrometres to 500 micrometres, respectively. 5. A multilayer film (100, 200, 300) of any of the preceding claims, characterized in that the functionalised graphene includes at least one of a functional group: aliphatic ester, aromatic ester, amine, epoxide, carboxyl, hydroxyl, siloxanes, silanes. 6. A multilayer film of any of the preceding claims, characterized in that concentration of the material selected from cellulose, polylactic acid and a polyhydroxyalkanoate mixed with the functionalised graphene may be in a range of 0.5 to 20 weight %. 7. A multilayer film of any of the preceding claims, characterized in that the substrate (104, 202) is polyethylene terephthalate. 8. A method for manufacturing a multilayer film (100) for packaging, the method comprising : - providing functionalised graphene; - mixing the functionalised graphene with a material selected from cellulose, polylactic acid and a polyhydroxyalkanoate; - dispersing the mixture of the functionalised graphene and the material in a solvent to form an ink (102); and - depositing a layer of ink (102) on a layer of substrate (104) to form the multilayer film (100). 9. A method of claim 8, characterized in that the method further includes applying a second layer of substrate (202) on the deposited layer of ink (102) to sandwich the deposited layer of ink (102) between the layers of substrate (104, 202). 10. A method of claim 9, characterized in that the method includes depositing a second layer of ink (302) between the second layer of substrate (202) and the deposited layer of ink (102). 11. A method of any one of claims 8 to 10, characterized in that each of the layers of ink (102, 302) and each of the layers of substrate (104, 202) includes a thickness in a range of 1 nanometres to 10 micrometres and a thickness in a range of 10 micrometres to 500 micrometres, respectively. 12. A method of any one of claims 8 to 11, characterized in that the method includes arranging for the functionalised graphene to include at least one of a functional group: aliphatic ester, aromatic ester, amine, epoxide, carboxyl, hydroxyl, siloxanes, silanes. 13. A method of any one of claims 8 to 12, characterized in that the method includes arranging for the solvent to further include a binder. 14. A method of claim 13, characterized in that the method includes arranging for the binder to include an organic compound. 15. A method of any one of the claims 8 to 14, characterized in that the method includes treating the substrate (104, 202) prior to deposition of the layer of ink (102, 302) thereon. 16. A method of claim 15, characterized in that the method includes treating the substrate (104, 202) includes at least one of: functionalizing, washing, coating. 17. A method of any of the claims 8 to 16, characterized in that the depositing the layer of ink (102, 302) on the layer of substrate (104, 202) is implemented by one of: spin-coating, bar-spreading or spraying.
